metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4923
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2A-B: A-Type K+ Current Density Gradient Is Abolished in DPP6-KO CA1 Dendrites.
In panel A&B, authors show that DPP6-KO mice lack a dendritic A-type K+ current gradient using dendritic patch clamp recordings in (A) CA1 pyramidal neurons in acute hippocampal slices and (B) in cell-attached recordings from CA1 dendrites.

Figure 2C-G: Kv2.4 recruitment to the distal dendrites is reduced in DPP6-KO mice compared to WT. This is demonstrated using (C-D) western blot of WT and DPP6-KO slices, (E-F) immunohistochemical labelling in sagittal hippocampal slices of WT and DPP6-KO mice, and (G) immunogold localization of Kv4.2 in the CA1 stratum radiatum of the hippocampus of WT and DPP6-KO mice

Figure 5: current clamp experiments in dendritic whole-cell recordings of CA1 hippocampal neurons show that dendritic excitability is enhanced in DPP6-KO CA1 pyramidal neurons by demonstrating that in (A) back-propagating action potentials recorded in distal CA1 DPP6-KO dendrites were larger in amplitude than those recorded in dendrites from WT mice and in (C) that bAP amplitude exhibits a relatively small activity-dependent decrease in DPP6-KO.

Figure 7: The pike-timing window for LTP Induction Is extended in DPP6-KO Mice, shown in: (E) an LTP magnitude - timing plot revealed that an expanded time window for LTP induction exists in DPP6-KO mice, (G) Compared with WT, DPP6-KO mice exhibited relatively larger single-burst integrals in the 36-40 and 41-45 ms time windows, indicating an extension of the spike-timing window of LTP induction.

Given the demonstrated regulatory activity of DPP6 over the Kv2.4 channel, which influences the postsynaptic membrane potential, combined with the changes on dendritic excitability in the DPP6-KO model, the GO-term 'regulation of postsynaptic membrane potential' was selected.. Experimental description: "A targeting vector was "knocked in" to the DPP6 locus via homologous recombination. Resulting mice were then crossed with mice expressing Cre-recombinase in germline cells causing recombination between the loxP sites flanking exon 2 and loss of the intervening genomic sequence"

"Hippocampal slices (250 μm thick) were prepared from 6- to 8-week-old male B6 WT and DPP6-KO mice"

Antibody production (from Clark et al., 2008): "The extracellular domain of human DPP6-L (residues 117-865) was cloned into the pAcGP67A vector (Pharmingen) in frame with the gp67 secretion sequence. A His10 tag was added at the C terminus for affinity purification. The purified recombinant DPP6 protein was injected into rabbits for the generation of polyclonal antibodies"

"PCR genotyping was performed using standard methods with primers DPP6F1 5'-TCGCTCTTGGCAGTCTGAA-3' and DPP6B1 5'-AATAGTATCATGAAATCCAGAACC-3' to yield PCR products of 377 bp for WT and 135 bp for KO alleles."
